vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely invalidated by the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) and P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are not visible as living specimens. Only P3 (Pothos/Rabbit) shows consistent presence.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected in black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: Pothos and White Rabbit anchor intact.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected in black pot.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Empty soil surface; no signs of Mexican Mint or Silver Guest.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos remains in a state of dormancy or severe senescence; White Rabbit anchor is stable.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Soil appears consistently dark and potentially over-saturated or hydrophobic; no visible fungal blooms.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Presence of a spoon (T-3) and a cylindrical object (T-2, T-1) indicates human intervention/maintenance tools.

temporal_deltas.T_earliest_to_T_minus_3: Introduction of a spoon into the yellow pot area; potential soil disturbance.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_3_to_T_minus_2: Removal of spoon; introduction of a cylindrical object (likely a sensor or probe) into the yellow pot.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_2_to_T_minus_1: Cylindrical object remains; no visible change to plant status.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_1_to_current: Removal of the cylindrical object; lighting conditions have degraded to near-total darkness, obscuring fine detail.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical failure. The absence of foliage in P1, P2, and P4 suggests total specimen loss. P3 is the only remaining biological anchor, though its health cannot be confirmed due to extreme low-light conditions in the current frame.
anomalies: Total loss of visible plant matter in P1, P2, and P4.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic decline in the botanical registry. Over the 5-day observation period, the pots P1, P2, and P4 have transitioned from potential growth sites to empty, soil-filled containers. The intervention of a spoon and a sensor probe suggests an attempt to monitor or revive the soil, but no vegetative recovery has occurred. The current image is severely underexposed, rendering a definitive health check on the remaining P3 specimen impossible, though it is the only pot retaining its structural anchor.
